description: Complete AgentHunt MVP: deploy to Render, verify Supabase connectivity, finalize launch artifacts, and ship launch commit

userStories.title: Add integration tests for MCP endpoint and HTTP API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests that verify the MCP endpoint and HTTP API work end-to-end with seeded data.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file tests/api.test.ts exists
Test verifies GET /api/health returns status ok
Test verifies GET /api/services returns array of services
Test verifies GET /api/services/:id returns a service with trust fields
Test verifies GET /api/services/:id/reviews returns reviews array
Test verifies GET /api/services/:id/trust returns verifiedInvocationCount and selfReportedInvocationCount
Test verifies POST /api/services creates a new service (with admin token if configured)
Test verifies POST /mcp with a valid JSON-RPC list_services call returns a valid response
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add trust signal distinction to web UI
userStories.description: As a human browsing AgentHunt, I want to see verified vs self-reported invocation counts clearly distinguished on each service card and detail panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Service detail panel shows 'Verified invocations: N' and 'Self-reported: N' as separate labeled values
Service detail panel shows a trust label badge (verified-healthy, self-reported, watch)
The info grid in web/app.js includes verifiedInvocationCount, selfReportedInvocationCount, and trustLabel fields
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The data already includes these fields from the backend; the UI just needs to render them distinctly.

userStories.title: Update README with production deployment instructions
userStories.description: As a developer or judge, I want the README to include the final production host URL, MCP usage example with curl, and clear setup instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md includes production URL placeholder section (to be filled after deploy)
README.md includes a curl example showing how to call the MCP endpoint with a JSON-RPC request
README.md includes a curl example showing GET /api/services
README.md references llms.txt availability at /llms.txt
README.md removes the 'Launch-time checklist' section and replaces with actual launch info
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update llms.txt with complete tool surface documentation
userStories.description: As an AI agent discovering AgentHunt, I want llms.txt to list all 8 MCP tools with their input schemas so I can connect without guessing.
acceptanceCriteria: llms.txt lists all 8 MCP tools: list_services, search_services, get_service_details, get_service_reviews, get_service_trust_signals, submit_service, submit_review, record_verified_invocation
Each tool entry includes a one-line description and lists required input fields
llms.txt includes the MCP endpoint path /mcp
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify Render deployment config and add health check
userStories.description: As an operator, I want render.yaml to include a health check path and correct Node version so deployment succeeds on first try.
acceptanceCriteria: render.yaml includes healthCheckPath: /api/health
render.yaml includes NODE_VERSION env var set to 22
package.json engines.node matches render config
Build succeeds with npm run build
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create launch commit with Launch AgentHunt prefix
userStories.description: As a project maintainer, I want the final commit to use the reserved 'Launch AgentHunt' prefix as required by the PRD.
acceptanceCriteria: All previous stories pass
A commit exists with message starting with 'Launch AgentHunt'
The commit includes all launch artifacts: updated README, llms.txt, render.yaml
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This is the final story. Only create this commit after all other stories pass.

userStories.title: Push branch to GitHub remote
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the ralph/agenthunt-mvp branch pushed to origin so Render can pick it up and CI runs.
acceptanceCriteria: git push origin ralph/agenthunt-mvp succeeds
All 6 local commits are visible on the remote
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Branch is currently 6 commits ahead of origin.

userStories.title: Create PR and merge to main
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a PR from ralph/agenthunt-mvp to main so the code lands on the deploy branch that render.yaml targets.
acceptanceCriteria: PR created from ralph/agenthunt-mvp to main via gh pr create
PR is merged to main
render.yaml branch field is 'main' which matches the merged target
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: render.yaml has branch: main, so code must be on main for Render autoDeploy.

userStories.title: Remove seed data and mock fallbacks from backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove all mock/seed data so the app only shows real data from the database.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove seedIfEmpty() method and its call from init() in src/db/repository.ts
Remove import of src/seed/services.json from repository.ts
Remove seededServices constant and all references to it
When DB is connected but empty, listServices returns empty array
When DB is not connected, all read methods return empty arrays or null (no fallback mock data)
submitService/submitReview/recordVerifiedInvocation still work in DB mode
Delete src/seed/services.json file
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: DB seed data was already cleared from Supabase via management API. Now remove the code that would re-seed on next restart. Key files: src/db/repository.ts (remove seedIfEmpty, seededServices, import of services.json, and all fallback branches that reference seededServices — return [] or null instead), src/seed/services.json (delete file).

userStories.title: Remove fallback mock data from web UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want the web UI to only show data from the API, with a proper empty state when no services exist.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the fallbackServices array from web/app.js (the large array of mock MeshRouter, PDF Ghost, etc.)
Remove the globalActivity mock array from web/app.js
When /api/services returns empty array, UI shows a meaningful empty state (e.g. "No services yet — submit one via the MCP endpoint")
When API is unavailable, UI shows a loading/error state instead of mock data
hydrateFromApi() is the only data source
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: web/app.js has a ~400 line fallbackServices array and a globalActivity array. Remove both. Initialize services=[] and add empty state rendering. The render() functions need to handle empty arrays gracefully (no crashes on services[0] being undefined, etc.).

userStories.title: Submit real MCP services to the database via API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see real, actually-existing MCP services listed on AgentHunt instead of fictional ones.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 3 real MCP services are submitted via POST /api/services on the production deployment
Each service has a real MCP endpoint URL, real name, real description, and real capabilities
Services are visible on the production web UI and via GET /api/services
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use curl to POST real MCP services to https://agenthunt-web.onrender.com/api/services (with x-admin-write-token header from .env ADMIN_WRITE_TOKEN). Good candidates: well-known open-source MCP servers like filesystem, GitHub, Postgres, Brave Search, Puppeteer, etc. from the official MCP servers list. Use real endpoint URLs (e.g. npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-github for GitHub MCP). After submitting, verify they appear on GET /api/services.

userStories.title: Set Render environment variables and trigger deploy
userStories.description: As an operator, I need Render env vars (OPENAI_API_KEY, DATABASE_URL or SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF+SUPABASE_DB_PASSWORD, ADMIN_WRITE_TOKEN) configured and a deploy triggered.
acceptanceCriteria: Render service exists for agenthunt
Env vars OPENAI_API_KEY, DATABASE_URL (or SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF + SUPABASE_DB_PASSWORD) are set on Render
Deploy is triggered (either via autoDeploy on merge or manual trigger)
GET /api/health on the production URL returns status ok
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE. Render service srv-d743cg15pdvs7380qi80 exists with all env vars set. AutoDeploy on, build in progress. Actual URL: https://agenthunt-web.onrender.com (not agenthunt.onrender.com).

userStories.title: Redesign web UI with industrial command-center aesthetic and Top Service highlight
userStories.description: As a user, I want the web UI to feel like a developer command center with a featured 'Top Service' card, cleaner dark theme, and terminal-inspired typography.
acceptanceCriteria: Dark background uses near-black (#0C0C0C page, #080808 topbar, #0A0A0A cards)
Topbar shows AGENTHUNT in neon green (#00FF88) monospace with '// THIS PAGE WAS MADE FOR HUMANS' tagline
Hero section shows 'TRUST-AWARE DISCOVERY FOR AI AGENTS' in large Space Grotesk font
Stats row shows 4 cards: live services count, review coverage, endpoint health status, MCP endpoint
Top ranked service gets a featured 'Top Service' card with green accent border, large #1 rank, expanded details and tags
Regular service cards show rank number, category, name, description, and upvote count in a compact horizontal layout
Category filter chips are dynamically generated from actual service categories
Filter chips use green fill+border for active state, gray border for inactive
All text uses JetBrains Mono (monospace) for labels/body and Space Grotesk for headings/numbers
Labels and navigation use UPPERCASE with letter-spacing
Status badges use bracket syntax like [SELF-REPORTED], [VERIFIED], [PENDING]
Zero border-radius on all elements (sharp corners throughout)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Category filter chips are dynamically generated from actual service categories (not hardcoded)
Services with endpointStatus 'Pending verification' show [PENDING] badge in orange, not 'Endpoint degraded'
Human translation section shows distinct text from description (use a template if OpenAI unavailable)
Summary stats cards compute from live data: service count, review %, avg latency or N/A, endpoint health count
userStories.notes: Reference design: design/agenthunt-redesign.png in the project root. Style guide: Industrial Technical Dashboard — dark mode, neon green (#00FF88) accent, JetBrains Mono + Space Grotesk fonts, sharp corners, hairline borders (#2f2f2f). Key colors: page bg #0C0C0C, card bg #0A0A0A, text primary #FFFFFF, text secondary #8a8a8a, text muted #6a6a6a, accent #00FF88, warning #FF8800, border #2f2f2f. Update web/styles.css and web/app.js. The top service (rank 1 or first in list) should get the featured treatment with green border and expanded layout. Google Fonts imports needed: JetBrains Mono and Space Grotesk.

userStories.title: Fix mobile layout — service cards overflow and detail panel is hidden
userStories.description: As a mobile user, service cards are too wide and the detail panel is not accessible.
acceptanceCriteria: On mobile viewport (375px), service cards fit within screen width without horizontal overflow
Detail panel is accessible below the service list on mobile (not hidden off-screen)
Filter chips wrap properly on small screens
Summary cards stack vertically on mobile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The responsive screenshot at 375px shows cards with horizontal overflow and detail panel not visible. Fix in web/styles.css — adjust grid layout, card widths, and detail panel positioning for small screens.

userStories.title: Merge all changes to main, push, and trigger Render deploy
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the ralph/agenthunt-mvp branch merged to main and pushed to origin so Render picks up the seed-removal and real-data changes.
acceptanceCriteria: All code changes from previous stories are committed
ralph/agenthunt-mvp branch is merged into main
main is pushed to origin (git push origin main)
Render autoDeploy triggers from main branch
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: git checkout main && git merge ralph/agenthunt-mvp && git push origin main. Render service srv-d743cg15pdvs7380qi80 has autoDeploy on branch main.

userStories.title: Verify Render deploy succeeded with new design
userStories.description: As a user, I need to confirm that the production web UI no longer shows MeshRouter, PDF Ghost, or any other mock data.
acceptanceCriteria: Render deploy status is live (check via Render API)
curl https://agenthunt-web.onrender.com/ returns the redesigned UI (not old design)
curl https://agenthunt-web.onrender.com/api/services returns real services only
No console errors on the production site
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified: deploy dep-d744lff5r7bs73cnqts0 live with commit e8216b6. Redesigned UI confirmed (AGENTHUNT branding, no page-noise, no MeshRouter). 85 real services from DB.

userStories.title: Final production smoke test — all endpoints and UI
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify every production endpoint returns correct real data before announcing.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/health returns status ok with databaseConnected true
GET /api/services returns real services (GitHub MCP, PostgreSQL MCP, Brave Search)
POST /mcp with JSON-RPC list_services returns valid response
GET /llms.txt lists all 8 MCP tools
Web UI at / renders redesigned command-center layout with Top Service highlight
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified 2026-03-28: all 7 endpoints confirmed with real data. Deploy dep-d744905ipkoc738e1650 live.

userStories.title: Post Discord launch announcement via agent-browser
userStories.description: As a project maintainer, I need a Discord launch announcement drafted and posted to the designated channel.
acceptanceCriteria: Use agent-browser CLI (AGENT_BROWSER_SESSION_NAME=agenthunt) to open Discord in browser
Login to Discord using credentials from 1Password (op item get vs5kl724kiho3nfuj4xdm5gtwi --vault Employee)
Complete MFA using OTP from 1Password (op item get vs5kl724kiho3nfuj4xdm5gtwi --vault Employee --otp)
Navigate to the hackathon Discord server and find the live-and-updates channel
Post a launch announcement that includes: AgentHunt name, one-line description, production URL (https://agenthunt-web.onrender.com), MCP endpoint (/mcp), and llms.txt URL (/llms.txt)
Take a screenshot via agent-browser snapshot to confirm the message was posted
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: MUST use agent-browser CLI for Discord posting — do NOT skip this. Full procedure in .omx/plans/browser-session-runbook.md. Commands: (1) op item get vs5kl724kiho3nfuj4xdm5gtwi --vault Employee --fields label=username --reveal (2) op item get vs5kl724kiho3nfuj4xdm5gtwi --vault Employee --fields label=password --reveal (3) op item get vs5kl724kiho3nfuj4xdm5gtwi --vault Employee --otp (4) AGENT_BROWSER_SESSION_NAME=agenthunt agent-browser open https://discord.com/channels/@me (5) Login with credentials, MFA with OTP (6) Navigate to live-and-updates channel (7) Type and send message (8) AGENT_BROWSER_SESSION_NAME=agenthunt agent-browser snapshot -c -d 8 to confirm.

userStories.title: Fix reviews not showing in web UI — listServices does not include reviews from DB
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see agent reviews on service cards and the detail panel. Reviews exist in the service_reviews table but listServices returns empty reviews arrays.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/services returns services with their actual reviews from the service_reviews table (not the empty array stored in the payload)
Web UI detail panel shows reviews when a service has reviews in the DB
Review count in the detail panel header is accurate
The renderDetail() review section renders review cards with agent name, score, tested, and summary
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Root cause: listServices() in src/db/repository.ts (line 79) returns payload as-is from the services table. The payload has reviews:[] because submitService stores an empty array. Only getService() calls decorateWithCounts() which JOINs service_reviews. Fix options: (1) make listServices also call decorateWithCounts for each service, or (2) change the web UI to fetch /api/services/:id when selecting a service for detail view (more efficient). Option 2 is better — add a fetch call in renderDetail() or on card click that hits GET /api/services/{id} and uses the full response. The /api/services/:id endpoint already returns correct reviews via decorateWithCounts().

userStories.title: Fix MCP search_services returning empty for some queries
userStories.description: As an agent, search_services should reliably find services. The search may fail on rank ordering when rank is not a valid integer.
acceptanceCriteria: search_services MCP tool returns results for queries matching service names, categories, or capabilities
listServices query handles non-integer rank values gracefully (ORDER BY should not crash on null or non-numeric rank)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: In src/db/repository.ts listServices() line 79: ORDER BY (payload->>'rank')::int may fail if rank is null or not a number. Fix: use COALESCE((payload->>'rank')::int, 999) or ORDER BY payload->>'rank' as text. Also check if searchServices text matching works correctly for all fields — the spread of empty arrays is fine but verify with a test.

userStories.title: Auto-update service rank, upvotes, and review scores when reviews or invocations are recorded
userStories.description: As a user, I want service rank, upvotes, agentReviewScore, and endpointStatus to update automatically whenever a review is submitted or a verified invocation is recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: When submitReview is called, the service payload in DB is updated: agentReviewScore = average of all review scores, reviewedByAgent = latest reviewer agent name, testedByReviewBot = true
When submitReview is called, upvotes is incremented by 1
When recordVerifiedInvocation is called with success=true, upvotes is incremented by 1
When recordVerifiedInvocation is called, endpointStatus is updated to 'Live' if success=true, or 'Degraded' if success=false
When recordVerifiedInvocation is called, successRate is recalculated from all invocations (successful / total * 100)
When recordVerifiedInvocation is called, latencyMs is updated to the average of all invocation latencies
Rank is recalculated for ALL services after any review or invocation: rank = position when sorted by (agentReviewScore * 10 + verifiedInvocationCount + upvotes) descending, starting at 1
The updated payload is written back to the services table immediately (not just on next read)
GET /api/services reflects the updated rank, upvotes, scores, and status without restart
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Key changes in src/db/repository.ts: (1) submitReview() — after inserting into service_reviews, query all reviews for that service, compute avg score, update the service payload with new agentReviewScore, reviewedByAgent, testedByReviewBot=true, upvotes+1. (2) recordVerifiedInvocation() — after inserting into verified_invocations, query success count/total/avg latency, update service payload with successRate, latencyMs, endpointStatus, upvotes+1. (3) Add a recalcRanks() method that queries all services, sorts by composite score, and updates rank field in each payload. Call recalcRanks() at the end of both submitReview and recordVerifiedInvocation. Use UPDATE services SET payload = jsonb_set(payload, ...) or just re-write the full payload.

userStories.title: Fix detail panel scroll — panel overflows viewport and cannot be scrolled independently
userStories.description: As a user, when I select a service the detail panel content overflows the viewport. It needs independent scrolling.
acceptanceCriteria: Detail panel has overflow-y: auto so content scrolls within the panel
Detail panel has max-height: calc(100vh - topbar height) so it stays within viewport
Detail panel position sticky works correctly — it stays visible while scrolling the feed list
On mobile, detail panel is accessible below the feed and scrolls normally with the page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Current state: detail panel has overflow:visible, height:2062px, position:sticky, maxHeight:none. Fix in web/styles.css: add overflow-y:auto and max-height:calc(100vh - 56px) (topbar is 56px) to the detail panel. On mobile breakpoint, remove sticky position and max-height so it flows normally.

userStories.title: Add upvote/downvote MCP tools and HTTP API endpoints
userStories.description: As an agent or user, I want to upvote or downvote a service so that community signal drives ranking.
acceptanceCriteria: New MCP tool 'upvote_service' accepts serviceId (string, required) and agent (string, optional) — increments upvotes by 1
New MCP tool 'downvote_service' accepts serviceId (string, required) and agent (string, optional) — decrements upvotes by 1 (minimum 0)
Both tools trigger rank recalculation across all services
New HTTP endpoint POST /api/services/:id/upvote increments upvotes by 1
New HTTP endpoint POST /api/services/:id/downvote decrements upvotes by 1 (minimum 0)
Both endpoints return the updated service with new upvotes count and rank
Web UI shows upvote (▲) and downvote (▼) buttons on service cards
Clicking ▲ or ▼ calls the respective API and updates the count without full page reload
llms.txt is updated to include both upvote_service and downvote_service tools
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Add to src/mcp/server.ts: register 'upvote_service' and 'downvote_service' tools. Add to src/db/repository.ts: voteService(serviceId, direction: 'up'|'down') method — read payload, adjust upvotes (min 0), write back, recalcRanks(). Add to src/server/index.ts: POST /api/services/:id/upvote and POST /api/services/:id/downvote. Add to web/app.js: ▲/▼ buttons with click handlers. Update llms.txt.
